item.title: U.S. regulators face July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules governing which issuers may operate domestically.
SEC plans to propose crypto safe-harbor rule in July 2026, exempting certain digital-asset offerings from securities registration.
European Commission opens MiCA review after July 1 transitional deadline, examining stablecoin interest ban and reserve requirements.
DTCC launches tokenized-securities pilot in July with BlackRock, Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan among 50+ participating firms.
Circle receives U.S. trust bank charter enabling affiliate custody; deposits, loans, and USDC reserve management remain excluded.
U.S. Digital Asset Market Clarity Act misses July Senate vote target; August 7 now the critical deadline for CFTC-SEC jurisdiction bill.
JPMorgan files to launch OnChain Liquidity-Token Money Market Fund on Ethereum, joining Wall Street's tokenized-fund race.
JPMorgan will offer clients Bitcoin purchasing access despite CEO Jamie Dimon's continued personal skepticism about the asset.
Interpol global fraud sweep arrests 5,811 individuals; one wallet linked to a 20-year-old suspect processed $122.5M in illicit funds.
Ethical hackers discover Aptos blockchain flaw that could have put $70B in assets at systemic risk; bug patched before exploitation.
Attacker drains $20M from memecoin DAO treasury by spending $4.4M on tokens to pass a malicious governance proposal.
Blockaid detects $6M exploit on Summer.fi DeFi platform, pausing Lazy Summer vaults and exposing risks in AI-automated yield strategies.
OCC publishes GENIUS Act compliance framework for stablecoin issuers, covering reserves, customer-ID requirements, and monthly disclosures.
U.S. Congress passes legislation blocking issuance of any central bank digital currency for the next four years.
Stablecoin supply rises $4B following GENIUS Act enactment as banks and asset managers enter the U.S. market.
